CREATE TABLE char_test (id INT, name CHAR(10));
INSERT INTO char_test VALUES (1, 'Alice');
INSERT INTO char_test VALUES (2, 'Bob');
INSERT INTO char_test VALUES (3, 'Charlie');
INSERT INTO char_test VALUES (4, 'David');
SELECT * FROM char_test WHERE LENGTH(name) = 5;
SELECT * FROM char_test WHERE name = 'Alice';
SELECT id FROM char_test WHERE name LIKE 'A%';
CREATE TABLE char_test_extended (id INT, details CHAR(50));
INSERT INTO char_test_extended SELECT id, CONCAT(name, ' - Extended') FROM char_test;
SELECT c1.id, c1.name, c2.details FROM char_test c1 JOIN char_test_extended c2 ON c1.id = c2.id;
SELECT id, name FROM char_test WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(name) < 10;
INSERT INTO char_test VALUES (5, 'Edward  ');
SELECT * FROM char_test WHERE LENGTH(name) > 5;
WITH filtered_chars AS (SELECT id, name FROM char_test WHERE LENGTH(name) BETWEEN 3 AND 6) SELECT * FROM filtered_chars;
CREATE TABLE char_test_long (id INT, name CHAR(2147483647));
INSERT INTO char_test_long VALUES (1, RPAD('X', 2147483647, ' '));
SELECT LENGTH(name) FROM char_test_long WHERE id = 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM char_test WHERE LENGTH(name) <= 10;
SELECT id FROM char_test WHERE name IN (SELECT name FROM char_test_extended WHERE LENGTH(details) > 20);
